title: RLHF and DPO at a glance
description: How preference optimization aligns a model to human-ranked outputs and where DPO simplifies the RLHF loop.
body: Use when the goal is aligning model behavior to human preference rather than teaching facts. RLHF trains a reward model on ranked pairs then optimizes the policy against it with a KL penalty to the reference; DPO skips the separate reward model by optimizing a preference loss directly on the pairs. Do not use preference tuning to inject knowledge — it shapes behavior, not facts. Actions: collect preference pairs, choose RLHF or DPO, and keep a KL/anchor to the base. Validation signals include a preference win-rate gain and a capability holdout that does not regress. Risk boundary: over-optimizing the reward causes reward hacking; if outputs game the metric, escalate and re-anchor to human review.
